contradiction_text: The benchmark reports substantial overall task success while also identifying environment configuration and deployment as the main bottlenecks, creating a tension between demonstrated capability and operational reliability.
related_claim_texts: Environment configuration and deployment are major failure points for current models on ABC-Bench.
On the reported evaluation setup, Claude Sonnet 4.5 achieved the best overall result at about 63.2% average Pass@1, and task success was strongly positively correlated with average agent turns.
disclosure: Keep visible that current models can solve many realistic backend tasks under the reported protocol, but still fail disproportionately at setup and deployment stages that determine end-to-end usability.

contradiction_text: The benchmark separates environment build from functional execution, which exposes a tension between coding/task logic competence and the ability to get systems running in the first place.
related_claim_texts: Environment configuration and deployment are major failure points for current models on ABC-Bench.
disclosure: Keep visible that benchmark failure cannot be read as a pure coding deficit, because some losses occur before functional correctness can be meaningfully tested.

contradiction_text: The strong positive correlation between average agent turns and task success is decision-relevant, but it sits in tension with any simple reading that more turns themselves cause better performance.
related_claim_texts: On the reported evaluation setup, Claude Sonnet 4.5 achieved the best overall result at about 63.2% average Pass@1, and task success was strongly positively correlated with average agent turns.
disclosure: Keep visible that the reported association is correlational under the stated protocol and should not be promoted into a causal claim that simply increasing turns will raise success.
